user:
The following Lean 4 proof attempt failed. Fix the proof.

Imports in scope: LeanSrc.Sorts_Mathlib.

### Input

* `<local_ctx>`: Local definitions from the same file that precede the target theorem. You may freely reference any of them without redefining them.
* `<broken_theorem>`: The theorem declaration and the proof body that produced an error.
* `<error>`: The Lean compiler error.

<local_ctx>
import Mathlib.Tactic.Linarith

--
-- Define correctness for sorting algorithms
--

-- list is sorted, i.e. no element is smaller than its predecessor
inductive Sorted [LT T] : List T -> Prop
  | nil : Sorted []
  | singleton : forall {x}, Sorted [x]
  | cons : forall {a b xs}, Sorted (b :: xs) -> Not (b < a) -> Sorted (a :: b :: xs)

-- counts the elements equal to t in l
def count1 [DecidableEq T] (l : List T) (t : T) : Nat :=
  match l with
  | [] => 0
  | x :: xs => (if x = t then 1 else 0) + count1 xs t

-- l and m are permutations of each other, i.e. they contain the same amount of each value
def Permut [DecidableEq T] (l m : List T) : Prop := forall t, count1 l t = count1 m t

-- a sorting algorithm is correct iff it each output is a sorted permutation of the input
def SortCorrect [Preorder T] [DecidableEq T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] (sort : List T -> List T) : Prop :=
  forall l, And (Sorted (sort l)) (Permut (sort l) l)

--
-- Lemmas for correctness propositions
--

def insert_ [LT T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] (t : T) : List T -> List T
  | [] => [t]
  | x :: xs => if t < x then t :: x :: xs else x :: insert_ t xs

def insertionsort [LT T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] : List T -> List T
  | [] => []
  | x :: xs => insert_ x (insertionsort xs)

--
-- Verify insertion sort
--
</local_ctx>

<broken_theorem>
theorem [Preorder T] [DecidableRel (LT.lt (α := T))] {l : List T} : Sorted (insertionsort l) := by
  let rec sorted_insert (t : T) : ∀ {l : List T}, Sorted l → Sorted (insert_ t l) := by
    intro l hs
    cases hs with
    | nil =>
        simp [insert_]
        exact Sorted.singleton
    | singleton =>
        rename_i x
        simp [insert_]
        by_cases h : t < x
        · simp [h]
          exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton h
        · simp [h]
          exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton h
    | cons hs_tail hba =>
        rename_i a b xs
        simp [insert_]
        by_cases hta : t < a
        · simp [hta]
          exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs_tail hba) hta
        · simp [hta]
          by_cases htb : t < b
          · simp [htb]
            exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs_tail htb) hba
          · simp [htb]
            exact Sorted.cons (sorted_insert t hs_tail) htb
  induction l with
  | nil =>
      simp [insertionsort]
      exact Sorted.nil
  | cons x xs ih =>
      simp [insertionsort]
      exact sorted_insert x ih
</broken_theorem>

response:
<reasoning>
The failures come from using `Sorted.cons` with a proof of `t < x` where the constructor requires `¬ x < t`. Under only a `Preorder`, this is obtained from `¬ x < t` by contradiction using transitivity with `t < x`. Similarly in the recursive `cons` case, when `t < a` we need `¬ a < t`, and when `t < b` we need `¬ b < t`.

The last error is structural: in the branch `¬ t < b`, `insert_ t (b :: xs)` reduces to `b :: insert_ t xs`, so we should recurse on `hs_tail : Sorted (b :: xs)` and then use that result directly, rather than trying to build an extra `Sorted.cons`.

A small helper `not_lt_of_lt` resolves the asymmetry using transitivity.
</reasoning>

<lean4_proof>
by
  have not_lt_of_lt : ∀ {a b : T}, a < b → ¬ b < a := by
    intro a b hab hba
    have haa : a < a := lt_trans hab hba
    exact lt_irrefl a haa
  let rec sorted_insert (t : T) : ∀ {l : List T}, Sorted l → Sorted (insert_ t l) := by
    intro l hs
    cases hs with
    | nil =>
        simp [insert_]
        exact Sorted.singleton
    | singleton =>
        rename_i x
        simp [insert_]
        by_cases h : t < x
        · simp [h]
          exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton (not_lt_of_lt h)
        · simp [h]
          exact Sorted.cons Sorted.singleton h
    | cons hs_tail hba =>
        rename_i a b xs
        simp [insert_]
        by_cases hta : t < a
        · simp [hta]
          exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs_tail hba) (not_lt_of_lt hta)
        · simp [hta]
          by_cases htb : t < b
          · simp [htb]
            exact Sorted.cons (Sorted.cons hs_tail (not_lt_of_lt htb)) hba
          · simp [htb]
            exact sorted_insert t hs_tail
  induction l with
  | nil =>
      simp [insertionsort]
      exact Sorted.nil
  | cons x xs ih =>
      simp [insertionsort]
      exact sorted_insert x ih
</lean4_proof>
